edges.description: Alertmanager forwards warning, info, and mirrored critical notifications into Mattermost channels, but operators can still inspect active alerts directly if chat delivery is degraded.
Critical paging depends on ntfy delivery, but Mattermost still provides a lower-fidelity fallback notification path.
The unified API gateway validates caller JWTs against Keycloak before serving authenticated platform routes.
Public access to api.lv3.org depends on the shared edge publication path, but the internal gateway remains reachable directly.
The changelog portal is served as generated static content on nginx-lv3.
Authenticated browser access to coolify.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, while the private controller remains reachable over the management network.
Public dashboard access to coolify.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication, while the private controller proxy remains reachable directly.
The shared applications ingress is configured and served by the Coolify runtime and its managed proxy.
Public access to apps.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication while the guest-local ingress remains reachable for direct verification.
Dify uses the governed API gateway route to call named LV3 tools, but the canvas remains reachable without tool invocation.
Operator browser access to Dify is gated behind the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak authentication flow at the edge.
Dify can publish workflow traces into Langfuse, but authoring and bootstrap access remain available without trace export.
Public reachability for agents.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Dify can use the private Ollama runtime for local model inference, but the canvas can still operate with external model providers.
Dify loads its mirrored database, admin, and tool-bridge secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Dify stores application state, accounts, knowledge metadata, and workflow definitions in Postgres.
The generated developer portal is published as static content on nginx-lv3.
Authenticated browser access to logs.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, but the private hub remains reachable for controlled operator verification.
Public operator access to logs.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication while the private runtime remains reachable inside the guest network.
The Excalidraw frontend and collaboration room are hosted on docker-runtime-lv3.
Operator access to draw.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak-backed oauth2-proxy edge flow, while the private runtime can still be verified locally.
The private diagram surface is published at draw.lv3.org through the shared NGINX edge, including the routed /socket.io/ collaboration path.
Gitea Actions jobs are delegated to the dedicated runner on docker-build-lv3, but repository hosting remains available without the runner.
Operator SSO into Gitea prefers Keycloak, while the repo-managed bootstrap admin remains the break-glass fallback.
Gitea loads its mirrored database, webhook, and OIDC client secrets from the OpenBao sidecar before startup.
Gitea persists repositories, metadata, and issue state in Postgres.
Grafana uses Keycloak for the preferred SSO path, but local admin remains the break-glass fallback.
Public access to grafana.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge; private reachability remains available if the edge is degraded.
Operator browser sign-in to registry.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak OIDC path, while robot credentials still support governed automation.
Public reachability for registry.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Public registration and control-plane reachability for headscale.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Public operator access to Homepage is intentionally gated through the shared Keycloak-backed oauth2-proxy flow on the NGINX edge.
The unified dashboard is published at home.lv3.org through the shared NGINX edge and is not exposed directly on the public internet.
Public reachability for sso.lv3.org depends on the shared edge publication path.
Keycloak reads its mirrored database and bootstrap secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Keycloak stores realm, client, and session state in Postgres.
Keycloak depends on the internal certificate authority when repo-managed internal TLS material is refreshed.
Keycloak provides the preferred OIDC browser sign-in path, but a repo-managed bootstrap user remains the break-glass fallback.
Public access to langfuse.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Langfuse starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Langfuse stores application metadata, users, projects, and bootstrap state in Postgres.
The published mail.lv3.org informational surface is exposed through the shared NGINX edge.
The mail gateway runtime is rendered from secrets mirrored locally by the OpenBao sidecar.
Public reachability for matrix.lv3.org depends on the shared edge publication path.
Matrix Synapse starts from mirrored database credentials rendered through the shared OpenBao compose environment helper.
Matrix Synapse stores users, room metadata, media references, and event history in Postgres.
Mattermost starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Mattermost stores channels, users, and message history in Postgres.
The public n8n editor path is gated behind oauth2-proxy and Keycloak-backed browser authentication.
Public reachability for n8n.lv3.org depends on the shared edge publication path.
n8n reads its mirrored database password and encryption key through the OpenBao agent before startup.
n8n stores workflow, credential, and execution state in Postgres.
Nextcloud stores account, sharing, file, and app metadata in Postgres.
Nextcloud receives its mirrored database, Redis, and admin bootstrap secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Public reachability for cloud.lv3.org and DAV discovery redirects depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
NetBox receives its database and Redis credentials from the OpenBao agent before the application containers start.
NetBox stores all inventory, topology, and IPAM data in Postgres.
Long-running internal helper services pinned to the build pool depend on the docker-build Nomad client remaining available for placement.
Runtime-targeted batch jobs and internal helper workloads depend on the docker-runtime Nomad client remaining available for placement.
Open WebUI can be configured for Keycloak OIDC, but the current runtime can still fall back to local login if OIDC is unavailable.
Open WebUI routes the repo-managed local model connector to the private Ollama runtime on docker-runtime-lv3.
Open WebUI consumes mirrored runtime secrets and optional OIDC client credentials from the OpenBao sidecar.
Open WebUI delegates optional web-search requests to the private SearXNG runtime when the feature is enabled.
ServerClaw prefers Keycloak-backed browser sign-in while retaining a repo-managed bootstrap admin as the break-glass fallback.
ServerClaw routes its default local model connector to the private Ollama runtime on docker-runtime-lv3.
ServerClaw consumes mirrored runtime secrets and the dedicated OIDC client secret from the OpenBao sidecar.
ServerClaw delegates optional web-search requests to the private SearXNG runtime when the feature is enabled.
Public access to chat.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
The interactive operations portal depends on the unified API gateway for governed platform actions and live status reads.
ops.lv3.org is gated behind oauth2-proxy and Keycloak-backed browser authentication.
The generated portal is published as static content on nginx-lv3.
Outline delegates routine browser sign-in to the shared Keycloak OIDC broker.
Public access to wiki.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Outline starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Outline stores workspace, user, and document metadata in Postgres.
Authenticated browser access to tasks.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, while the private controller path remains available for governed automation.
Public access to tasks.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Plane consumes mirrored runtime secrets from the OpenBao sidecar before the containers start.
Plane persists workspaces, projects, users, and issue metadata in Postgres.
The platform context API loads its bearer token and runtime configuration from OpenBao-rendered environment files.
The indexed corpus includes step-ca ADR and runbook content for operator query grounding.
Public operator access to realtime.lv3.org depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, but the private Netdata parent remains reachable on monitoring-lv3.
Public operator access to realtime.lv3.org depends on the shared NGINX edge publication while the private Netdata parent remains reachable on the guest network.
Semaphore loads database and bootstrap secrets from the OpenBao agent before the container starts.
Semaphore persists project metadata, inventories, templates, and task history in Postgres.
The public status page is published at status.lv3.org through the shared NGINX edge hostname.
The public status page is rendered by the Uptime Kuma runtime and cannot function if that service is unavailable.
Vaultwarden stores encrypted vault metadata and shared credential state in PostgreSQL.
Vaultwarden uses step-ca issued private TLS certificates for the Tailscale-only HTTPS surface and managed renewal path.
Windmill workers and the server runtime load mirrored secrets from the OpenBao agent before the containers start.
Windmill persists workflow metadata, run history, and state in Postgres.
